NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF
CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT SWINERTON INCORPORATED HARBISON-MAHONY-HIGGINS BUILDERS, INC. PRINCIPAL FINANCIAL GROUP JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 7.0 DAYS

LAW AND MOTION, 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT PANATTONI DEVELOPMENT COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION TO TRANSFER TO VENUE TO SACRAMENTO COUNTY IS DENIED. (1) PLAINTIFFS ARE ENTITLED TO BRING THE ACTION IN A COUNTY IN WHICH A DEFENDANT RESIDES, AND IT DOES NOT MATTER THAT OTHER DEFENDANTS RESIDE IN A DIFFERENT COUNTY. E.G., COLUSA AIR POLLUTION CONTROL DIST. V. SUPERIOR COURT, 226 CAL.APP.3D 880, 890 (1991). (2) AS AN ALTERNATIVE, MOVANT SUGGESTS TRANSFER OF VENUE TO ASSURE THE CONVENIENCE OF THE WITNESSES. CCP 397(C). PLAINTIFF'S CHOICE OF VENUE IS PRESUMPTIVELY CORRECT, LIEBERMAN V. SUPERIOR COURT, 194 CAL.APP.3D 396, 401 (1987), AND THE MOTION MUST EXPLAIN VIA A DECLARATION WHY FOR IDENTIFIED WITNESSES THE TRANSFER WOULD BE CONVENIENT. E.G., JUNEAU V. JUNEAU, 45 CAL.APP.2D 14, 15-17 (1941). NO SUCH SHOWING IS MADE OR INDEED ATTEMPTED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CURTIS E.A. KARNOW; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976

(Joinder) To Motion To Transfer To Venue To Sacramento County
Off Calendar Jul-23-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Richard H. Dalrymple, III, Esq. of the Law Offices of Larry S. Buckley, Tel. 530.343.3695, appearing for for Plaintiff and opposing party via court call.

No other appearance. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs SWINERTON INCORPORATED, HARBISON-MAHONY-HIGGINS BUILDERS, INC., PRINCIPAL FINANCIAL GROUP's (Joinder) To Motion To Transfer To Venue To Sacramento County - The ruling on the joinder is the same as on the underlying motion. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976

Notice Of Motion And Motion
Off Calendar Jul-23-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Richard H. Dalrymple, III, Esq. of the Law Offices of Larry S. Buckley, Tel. 530.343.3695, appearing for for Plaintiff and opposing party via court call.

No other appearance. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT PANATTONI DEVELOPMENT COMPANY, INC.'s Motion To Transfer To Venue To Sacramento County is Denied. (1) Plaintiffs are entitled to bring the action in a county in which a defendant resides, and it does not matter that other defendants reside in a different county. E.g., Colusa Air Pollution Control Dist. v. Superior Court, 226 Cal.App.3d 880, 890 (1991). (2) As an alternative, movant suggests transfer of venue to assure the convenience of the witnesses. CCP 397(c). Plaintiff's choice of venue is presumptively correct, Lieberman v. Superior Court, 194 Cal.App.3d 396, 401 (1987), and the motion must explain via a declaration why for identified witnesses the transfer would be convenient. E.g., Juneau v. Juneau, 45 Cal.App.2d 14, 15-17 (1941). No such showing is made or indeed attempted. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
